User.title: User
User.description: Stores user profile information, authentication-related flags, user role, and personal API keys for Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) functionality. Passwords and other authentication system data are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

displayName.description: The user's preferred display name or full name.

photoUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

role.description: The assigned role of the user (e.g., 'Admin', 'User').

hasClaimedCredit.description: Indicates whether the user has claimed the $300 Google Cloud credit offer.

geminiApiKey.description: The user's personal API key for the Gemini AI service, enabling Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) functionality.

elevenLabsApiKey.description: The user's personal API key for the ElevenLabs text-to-speech service, enabling BYOK functionality.

heyGenApiKey.description: The user's personal API key for the HeyGen video generation service, enabling BYOK functionality.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

File.title: File
File.description: Stores metadata for files uploaded by users for AI processing, including ownership, training data status, and an expiration timestamp for automatic deletion using Firestore TTL policies.

id.description: Unique identifier for the File entity.

ownerId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded and owns this file. (Relationship: User 1:N File)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileUrl.description: The public URL where the file is stored (e.g., Firebase Storage).

fileType.description: The MIME type of the uploaded file (e.g., 'image/jpeg', 'audio/mp3').

fileSize.description: The size of the file in bytes.

isTrainingData.description: Flag indicating if this file is designated for permanent AI model training. If false, it's subject to TTL deletion.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the file metadata (and associated file in storage) should be considered for deletion by a TTL policy. Only set if 'isTrainingData' is false.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the file was uploaded.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles, authentication-related flags, user role (for application logic/UI display), and personal API keys. Access is strictly limited to the document owner. Includes denormalized 'id' field matching 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores metadata for user-uploaded files, including ownership and expiration data. Each document includes an 'ownerId' field explicitly denormalized for authorization independence. Files flagged as 'isTrainingData: false' will have 'expiresAt' set for Firestore TTL deletion.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded file.

definition.description: A collection used for global admin role authorization. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{uid}` signifies that the user with `uid` has administrator privileges, following the 'Existence over Content' pattern for DBAC. This enables atomic rule checks without `get()` dependencies.

params.name: uid
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds the admin role.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

1.  **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`)**: Each user's profile information, including their API keys and credit claim status, is stored in a document `userId` within the top-level `users` collection. The `userId` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, making authorization for a user's *own* profile atomic and independent. Security rules can simply check `request.auth.uid == userId` without any `get()` operations, perfectly fulfilling Authorization Independence. This structure also supports QAPs as a user can only query their specific document, not list other users' profiles.

2.  **User Files (`/files/{fileId}`)**: Files uploaded by users are stored in a top-level `files` collection. Each `File` document explicitly includes an `ownerId` field. This `ownerId` is a denormalized attribute that directly references the user who owns the file. This is crucial for Authorization Independence (Mandate A) because security rules can directly check `resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid` to grant read/write access. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to a parent `User` document. This design also fully supports QAPs (Mandate QAPs) by allowing `list` operations on the `/files` collection where Firestore can efficiently filter documents based on the `ownerId` field, ensuring only files owned by the requesting user are returned.

3.  **Admin Roles (`/roles_admin/{uid}`)**: For global administrator roles, a separate `roles_admin` collection is used. An admin user's UID will exist as a document ID in this collection. This adheres to the 'Existence over Content' principle for DBAC (Mandate C.4) and ensures Authorization Independence. Security rules for global admin access can use a simple `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` check, which is atomic and does not incur `get()` dependencies. The `role` field within the `/users/{userId}` document is retained as requested for application-level logic or UI display, but `roles_admin` will be the definitive source for security rule-based global admin authorization.

This structure ensures clear, explicit authorization intent, simplifies security rules by removing complex `get()` dependencies, and provides robust support for secure `list` operations (QAPs) through appropriate segregation and denormalization.
